response:
Section 1  
Write a Python function to count the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The input will be a string containing the text and another string containing the word to search for. The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.  

Section 2  
The function should be case insensitive, so "Freedom" and "freedom" should be counted as the same word. The input will be a string containing the text and another string containing the word to search for. The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.  

Section 3  
The function should handle punctuation and other non-alphanumeric characters. The function should count the word even if it is surrounded by punctuation or at the beginning or end of the text. The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.  

Section 4  
The function should be efficient and handle large inputs without performance issues. The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.  

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. Mark the beginning of each section with Section X, such as:  
Section 1  
[content of section 1]  
Section 2  
[content of section 2]  
No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.
